meaning: adj.球根的,球根状的,球根长成的
examples: More than a thousand people claim to have seen the bulbous back of an unidentified creature briefly break the glassy surface of Loch Ness, then disappear.
Bulbous white heads glow atop the downtown street lamps.
Some say it wears a hat to hide its blowhole and its bulbous forehead.
Indeed those elegant, wafer thin bulbous stems are simply perfect for enjoying wine, but cleaning them?
Its rustic vibe and bulbous shape ground the bouquet and its opacity hides distracting stems.
